Most people write prompts like Google searches. But AI needs context like a new employee needs onboarding. The P in PROMPT = Precision. Be specific first. #PromptScoreChallenge
Secret to 90+ prompt scores: Role assignment. Don't just say 'write me an email.' Say 'You are a senior copywriter with 10 years in SaaS B2B marketing.' #PromptEngineering

The O in PROMPT = Output format. Without it your AI guesses what you want. Add: 'Format as a bullet list with headers' or 'Write as a 3-paragraph email.' Score jumps 15-20 pts. #AI

We built ScoreMyPrompt because prompt engineering shouldn't be guesswork. Our tool evaluates your AI prompts across 6 dimensions (Precision Role Output Format Mission Context Structure Tailoring) and gives you an instant score out of 100. After analyzing hundreds of prompts we found that 82% fail on the same dimension: Precision. Most people write vague instructions and wonder why AI gives generic answers. Try it free - no signup required: scoremyprompt.com #PromptEngineering #AI #ProductLaunch

The gap between a 40-point prompt and a 90-point prompt isn't creativity - it's structure. We analyzed prompt patterns across marketing developers product managers and designers. The pattern is clear: high-scoring prompts always define three things upfront: 1. WHO the AI should be (Role) 2. WHAT format you need (Output) 3. HOW specific the task is (Precision) These aren't advanced techniques. They're fundamentals that most people skip. Score your prompts and see where you stand: scoremyprompt.com #PromptEngineering #AIProductivity

The PROMPT framework: P-Precision R-Role O-Output Format M-Mission Context S-Structure T-Tailoring. Score yourself on each. Most people set a Role but completely forget Output Format.

Fun stat: the dimension most prompts fail on is Precision. People ask AI to 'write a blog post' instead of 'write a 800-word technical blog post about React Server Components for senior developers.' Precision = quality.
